rawText:
If there is anyone out there who still doubts that America is a place where all things are possible; who still wonders if the dream of our founders is alive in our time; who still questions the power of our democracy, tonight is your answer.

It's the answer told by lines that stretched around schools and churches in numbers this nation has never seen; by people who waited three hours and four hours, many for the very first time in their lives, because they believed that this time must be different; that their voice could be that difference.

It's the answer spoken by young and old, rich and poor, Democrat and Republican, black, white, Latino, Asian, Native American, gay, straight, disabled and not disabled — Americans who sent a message to the world that we have never been a collection of red states and blue states; we are, and always will be, the United States of America.

It's been a long time coming, but tonight, because of what we did on this day, in this election, at this defining moment, change has come to America.

I just received a very gracious call from Sen. McCain. He fought long and hard in this campaign, and he's fought even longer and harder for the country he loves. He has endured sacrifices for America that most of us cannot begin to imagine, and we are better off for the service rendered by this brave and selfless leader.

I would not be standing here tonight without the unyielding support of my best friend for the last 16 years, the rock of our family and the love of my life, our nation's next first lady, Michelle Obama.

But above all, I will never forget who this victory truly belongs to — it belongs to you.

I was never the likeliest candidate for this office. We didn't start with much money or many endorsements. Our campaign was not hatched in the halls of Washington — it began in the backyards of Des Moines and the living rooms of Concord and the front porches of Charleston.

It was built by working men and women who dug into what little savings they had to give $5 and $10 and $20 to this cause.

The road ahead will be long. Our climb will be steep. We may not get there in one year, or even one term, but America — I have never been more hopeful than I am tonight that we will get there. I promise you: We as a people will get there.

This is our chance to answer that call. This is our moment. This is our time — to put our people back to work and open doors of opportunity for our kids; to restore prosperity and promote the cause of peace; to reclaim the American Dream and reaffirm that fundamental truth that out of many, we are one; that while we breathe, we hope, and where we are met with cynicism, and doubt, and those who tell us that we can't, we will respond with that timeless creed that sums up the spirit of a people: Yes, we can.

overview.title: Barack Obama 2008 Victory Speech
overview.source: 2008 Presidential Election Victory Speech, Grant Park, Chicago

sentences.original: If there is anyone out there who still doubts that America is a place where all things are possible; who still wonders if the dream of our founders is alive in our time; who still questions the power of our democracy, tonight is your answer.
sentences.translation: 아직도 미국이 모든 것이 가능한 곳이라는 것을 의심하는 사람이 있다면; 우리 건국의 아버지들의 꿈이 우리 시대에 살아 있는지 여전히 궁금해하는 사람이 있다면; 우리 민주주의의 힘을 여전히 의문시하는 사람이 있다면, 오늘 밤이 여러분의 답입니다.

grammarElements.text: If there is anyone out there
grammarElements.label: 조건 부사절 (if)

grammarElements.text: who still doubts that America is a place where all things are possible
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who) + that절 + where절

grammarElements.text: who still wonders if the dream of our founders is alive in our time
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who) + 명사절 (if)

grammarElements.text: who still questions the power of our democracy
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who)

vocabulary.example: I never doubted his ability.
The founders of the nation wrote the Constitution.
Democracy requires active participation from citizens.

expressions.usage: 불특정 다수의 청중에게 호소할 때
과거의 이상이 현재에도 유효한지를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: If there is anyone who [A]; who [B]; who [C], [conclusion]
patterns.explanation: 세 개의 who 관계절을 세미콜론으로 연결하는 병렬 구조. 반복과 점층법으로 극적 효과를 높인다.
examples: If there is anyone who doubts our ability; who questions our resolve; who underestimates our unity, let them watch what we do next.

drillQuestions.question: If there is anyone _____ still believes it cannot be done, tonight proves them wrong.

sentences.notes: 연설 전체의 첫 문장이자 가장 유명한 구절. if 조건절 안에 who 관계대명사가 세 번 반복되며, 각 who절 안에 that절, where절, if명사절이 중첩된 매우 복잡한 다층 구조. 점층법(climax)의 전형적 예.

koreanAlignment.en: If there is anyone out there
who still doubts that America is a place
who still wonders if the dream of our founders is alive
koreanAlignment.ko: 건국의 아버지들의 꿈이 살아 있는지 궁금해하는

koreanAlignment.en: who still questions the power of our democracy

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삼중 반복(tricolon) + 점층법(climax) — who 절을 세 번 반복하며 의미를 고조시킴

sentences.original: It's the answer told by lines that stretched around schools and churches in numbers this nation has never seen; by people who waited three hours and four hours, many for the very first time in their lives, because they believed that this time must be different; that their voice could be that difference.
sentences.translation: 그것은 이 나라가 한 번도 본 적 없는 수의 사람들이 학교와 교회 주위에 줄을 서서 알려준 답이며; 세 시간, 네 시간을 기다린 사람들, 많은 이들이 생에 처음으로 기다린 사람들이 알려준 답입니다, 왜냐하면 그들은 이번에는 달라야 한다고 믿었기 때문입니다; 자신의 목소리가 그 차이가 될 수 있다고.

grammarElements.text: It's the answer told by lines
grammarElements.label: 주절 (과거분사 수식)

grammarElements.text: that stretched around schools and churches
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that)

grammarElements.text: in numbers this nation has never seen
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사 생략 (numbers that this nation...)

grammarElements.text: by people who waited three hours and four hours
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 + 관계대명사절 (who)

grammarElements.text: because they believed that this time must be different
grammarElements.label: 이유 부사절 (because) + that절

grammarElements.text: that their voice could be that difference
grammarElements.label: 동격절 (that)

vocabulary.example: The line stretched around the block.
Every citizen deserves a voice in democracy.

expressions.usage: 최초의 경험을 강조할 때. very가 first를 강조.
변화에 대한 확신이나 요구를 표현할 때

patterns.pattern: It's the answer told by [group A]; by [group B], because they believed that [belief]
patterns.explanation: 반복 구조(told by ... by ...)로 여러 집단을 병렬 나열하고 because로 동기를 설명하는 패턴.
examples: It's the victory won by the workers; by the volunteers, because they believed that change was possible.

drillQuestions.question: It's the message delivered by teachers; _____ students who wanted a better future.

sentences.notes: "lines"는 투표를 위해 줄 선 사람들. "numbers this nation has never seen"에서 관계대명사 that이 생략됨. 구체적 이미지(줄, 기다림, 시간)로 추상적 개념(민주주의)을 감각적으로 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: It's the answer told by lines
that stretched around schools and churches
in numbers this nation has never seen
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 나라가 한 번도 본 적 없는 수의

koreanAlignment.en: by people who waited three hours and four hours
koreanAlignment.ko: 세 시간, 네 시간을 기다린 사람들이

koreanAlignment.en: many for the very first time in their lives
because they believed that this time must be different
that their voice could be that difference
koreanAlignment.ko: 자신의 목소리가 그 차이가 될 수 있다고

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 구체적 이미지(concrete imagery) — 줄서기, 기다림이라는 구체적 장면으로 민주주의를 시각화

sentences.original: It's the answer spoken by young and old, rich and poor, Democrat and Republican, black, white, Latino, Asian, Native American, gay, straight, disabled and not disabled — Americans who sent a message to the world that we have never been a collection of red states and blue states; we are, and always will be, the United States of America.
sentences.translation: 그것은 젊은이와 노인, 부자와 가난한 자, 민주당원과 공화당원, 흑인, 백인, 라틴계, 아시아계, 아메리카 원주민, 동성애자와 이성애자, 장애인과 비장애인 — 세상에 우리가 결코 적색 주와 청색 주의 모임이 아니었다는 메시지를 보낸 미국인들이 말한 답입니다; 우리는 미합중국이었고, 언제나 미합중국일 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: It's the answer spoken by young and old, rich and poor, ...
grammarElements.label: 주절 + 과거분사 수식 + 대구 나열

grammarElements.text: Americans who sent a message to the world
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 관계대명사절 (who)

grammarElements.text: that we have never been a collection of red states and blue states
grammarElements.label: 동격절 (that)

grammarElements.text: we are, and always will be, the United States of America
grammarElements.label: 주절 (삽입구 포함)

vocabulary.example: The museum has a large collection of paintings.
The building has access for disabled visitors.

expressions.meaning: 공화당 지지 주와 민주당 지지 주
expressions.usage: 미국 정치에서 정치적 분열을 나타내는 표현

expressions.phrase: sent a message to the world
expressions.usage: 집단적 행동이 상징적 의미를 가질 때

patterns.explanation: 대조되는 쌍을 병렬 나열한 후 대시(—)로 전체를 아우르는 집단을 제시하는 패턴. 통합의 메시지를 강조.
examples: Teachers and students, parents and children — citizens who believe in a better future.

drillQuestions.question: Young and old, rich and poor — _____ who came together for a common cause.

sentences.notes: 미국 사회의 다양성을 대조쌍으로 나열한 후 "United States"로 통합하는 수사적 대비. "red states and blue states" vs "United States"의 말장난(pun). 가장 유명한 구절 중 하나.

koreanAlignment.en: young and old, rich and poor
black, white, Latino, Asian, Native American
gay, straight, disabled and not disabled
Americans who sent a message to the world
that we have never been a collection of red states and blue states
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리가 적색 주와 청색 주의 모임이 아니었다는

koreanAlignment.en: we are, and always will be, the United States of America

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대조쌍 나열(antithetical pairs) + 말장난(pun) — red/blue states vs United States의 이중 의미

sentences.original: It's been a long time coming, but tonight, because of what we did on this day, in this election, at this defining moment, change has come to America.
sentences.translation: 오랫동안 기다려 왔지만, 오늘 밤, 이 날에 우리가 한 일 때문에, 이 선거에서, 이 결정적인 순간에, 변화가 미국에 찾아왔습니다.

grammarElements.text: It's been a long time coming
grammarElements.label: 주절 (현재완료 진행)

grammarElements.text: because of what we did on this day, in this election, at this defining moment
grammarElements.label: 이유 전치사구 (because of + what절)

vocabulary.example: The battle was a defining moment in the war.

expressions.phrase: it's been a long time coming
expressions.usage: 오래 기다린 일이 마침내 실현되었을 때
집단적 행동의 결과를 강조할 때. because of + what 관계절.

patterns.pattern: It's been a long time coming, but [result]
patterns.explanation: 오래된 기다림과 그 끝에 온 결과를 대비하는 패턴. 역사적 성취를 강조.
examples: It's been a long time coming, but we finally achieved our goal.

drillQuestions.question: It's been a long time _____, but justice has finally arrived.

sentences.notes: "on this day, in this election, at this defining moment"는 전치사구의 삼중 반복(tricolon). 시간→사건→의미로 범위를 좁혀가는 점층 구조. "change has come"은 캠페인 슬로건 "Change"의 최종 실현.

koreanAlignment.en: It's been a long time coming
on this day, in this election, at this defining moment
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 날에, 이 선거에서, 이 결정적인 순간에

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삼중 반복(tricolon) — "on this day, in this election, at this defining moment"로 점층적 강조

sentences.original: He fought long and hard in this campaign, and he's fought even longer and harder for the country he loves.
sentences.translation: 그는 이 선거에서 오랫동안 열심히 싸웠고, 자신이 사랑하는 나라를 위해 더 오래 더 열심히 싸워왔습니다.

grammarElements.text: he's fought even longer and harder
grammarElements.label: 동사 (현재완료 비교급)

vocabulary.example: The presidential campaign lasted over a year.
She fought hard for justice.

expressions.usage: 노력과 헌신의 정도를 강조할 때
even으로 비교급을 강조하는 표현

patterns.explanation: 과거형에서 현재완료 비교급으로 확대하며 더 큰 헌신을 강조하는 패턴.
examples: She worked hard in school, and she's worked even harder in her career.

drillQuestions.question: He ran fast in practice, and he's run _____ faster in the actual race.

sentences.notes: 패배한 상대(매케인)를 존중하는 화법. "long and hard → even longer and harder"로 비교급을 점층시키며 상대의 헌신을 높인다. "the country he loves"에서 관계대명사 that 생략.

koreanAlignment.en: and he's fought even longer and harder
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 더 오래 더 열심히 싸워왔습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층 비교(gradatio) — long and hard → even longer and harder로 헌신의 깊이를 단계적으로 높임

sentences.original: He has endured sacrifices for America that most of us cannot begin to imagine, and we are better off for the service rendered by this brave and selfless leader.
sentences.translation: 그는 우리 대부분이 상상조차 할 수 없는 미국을 위한 희생을 견뎌왔으며, 이 용감하고 헌신적인 지도자가 행한 봉사 덕분에 우리는 더 나은 삶을 살고 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: that most of us cannot begin to imagine
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (that)

grammarElements.text: for the service rendered by this brave and selfless leader
grammarElements.label: 이유 전치사구 + 과거분사 수식

vocabulary.example: The soldiers endured harsh conditions.
Freedom requires great sacrifice.
Her selfless devotion inspired everyone.
He rendered great service to the country.

expressions.usage: 어떤 것이 경험 밖의 것임을 강하게 표현할 때
누군가의 기여 덕분에 상황이 개선되었음을 표현할 때
과거분사의 후치수식. 격식적 문어체에서 사용.

patterns.pattern: [Subject] has endured [noun] that most of us cannot [verb]
patterns.explanation: 현재완료 + 관계대명사 that절로 경험의 깊이를 강조하는 패턴.
examples: She has faced challenges that most of us cannot comprehend.

drillQuestions.question: He has endured hardships _____ most people cannot even imagine.

sentences.notes: "service rendered by"에서 rendered는 과거분사의 후치수식(격식 영어). "cannot begin to imagine"은 상상할 수 없다를 더 강하게 표현하는 관용 표현. 매케인의 전쟁 포로 경험을 암시.

koreanAlignment.en: He has endured sacrifices for America
that most of us cannot begin to imagine
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 대부분이 상상조차 할 수 없는
그리고 우리는 더 나은 삶을 살고 있습니다

koreanAlignment.en: by this brave and selfless leader

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 경의 표현(tribute) — 패배한 상대의 헌신을 격식 높은 언어로 인정

sentences.original: I would not be standing here tonight without the unyielding support of my best friend for the last 16 years, the rock of our family and the love of my life, our nation's next first lady, Michelle Obama.
sentences.translation: 지난 16년간 저의 가장 친한 친구이자, 우리 가족의 반석이며 제 인생의 사랑인, 우리나라의 차기 영부인 미셸 오바마의 굳건한 지지 없이는 오늘 밤 여기 서 있지 못했을 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: without the unyielding support of my best friend
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (조건의 without)

grammarElements.text: the rock of our family and the love of my life, our nation's next first lady, Michelle Obama
grammarElements.label: 동격 (세 개의 수식어)

vocabulary.example: Her unyielding determination led to success.
She has been the rock of our family.

expressions.usage: 가정법으로 누군가의 기여 없이는 불가능했음을 표현
가족의 중심이자 지주를 비유적으로 표현
배우자나 연인에 대한 깊은 사랑을 표현

patterns.explanation: 가정법으로 감사를 표현하는 패턴. "~없이는 지금 ~하고 있지 못할 것이다."
examples: I would not be here today without the support of my parents.
She would not be succeeding without her mentor.

drillQuestions.question: I would not be standing here tonight _____ your help.

sentences.notes: 가정법 과거(would not be standing)로 미셸 오바마에 대한 감사를 표현. 동격 구문이 세 겹으로 중첩(best friend → rock → love → first lady → Michelle Obama)되어 이름을 마지막에 공개하는 극적 효과.

koreanAlignment.en: I would not be standing here tonight
koreanAlignment.ko: 오늘 밤 여기 서 있지 못했을 것입니다

koreanAlignment.en: of my best friend for the last 16 years
and the love of my life
our nation's next first lady, Michelle Obama

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 지연 공개(delayed reveal) — 수식어를 쌓아가며 이름을 마지막에 밝히는 극적 구조

sentences.original: But above all, I will never forget who this victory truly belongs to — it belongs to you.
sentences.translation: 하지만 무엇보다도, 이 승리가 진정 누구의 것인지 저는 결코 잊지 않을 것입니다 — 그것은 여러분의 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: who this victory truly belongs to
grammarElements.label: 명사절 (의문사절)

vocabulary.example: Above all, we must remain united.
The team celebrated their hard-won victory.
I am truly grateful for your help.

expressions.usage: 가장 중요한 것을 강조할 때
소유나 귀속을 표현할 때

patterns.explanation: 의문사절로 궁금증을 만든 후 대시(—)로 답을 즉시 제공하는 극적 패턴.
examples: I will never forget what she taught me — kindness above all.
We must always remember who made this possible — the volunteers.

drillQuestions.question: I will never forget _____ this opportunity came from — it came from your support.

sentences.notes: 대시(—) 뒤에 "it belongs to you"를 배치하여 극적 전환. 연설의 핵심 메시지(승리는 시민의 것)를 간결하게 전달. 영어 연설의 청중 참여 기법.

koreanAlignment.en: who this victory truly belongs to

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 극적 전환(dramatic pivot) — 대시 뒤에 핵심 메시지를 배치하여 강한 인상을 남김

sentences.original: I was never the likeliest candidate for this office.
sentences.translation: 저는 이 직책에 가장 유력한 후보가 아니었습니다.

vocabulary.example: She was the likeliest winner of the competition.
There are three candidates for the position.
She was elected to public office.

patterns.explanation: "저는 결코 가장 ~한 ~가 아니었다." 겸손함을 표현하면서도 성취의 의미를 부각.
examples: I was never the smartest student in class.
She was never the fastest runner on the team.

drillQuestions.question: He was never the _____ likely person to succeed, but he did.

sentences.notes: 겸손함과 역경 극복의 서사를 시작하는 문장. "office"는 여기서 대통령 직위를 의미. "likeliest"는 likely의 최상급.

sentences.original: Our campaign was not hatched in the halls of Washington — it began in the backyards of Des Moines and the living rooms of Concord and the front porches of Charleston.
sentences.translation: 우리의 선거 운동은 워싱턴의 복도에서 부화된 것이 아닙니다 — 디모인의 뒤뜰과 콩코드의 거실과 찰스턴의 앞 현관에서 시작되었습니다.

grammarElements.text: it began in the backyards of Des Moines and the living rooms of Concord and the front porches of Charleston
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (삼중 병렬)

vocabulary.example: They hatched a plan to change the system.
They sat on the front porch and watched the sunset.
The children played in the backyard.

expressions.usage: 권력의 중심에서 만들어졌음을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] was not [verb]ed in [elite place] — it began in [ordinary places]
patterns.explanation: 부정과 긍정을 대비하여 풀뿌리(grassroots) 기원을 강조하는 패턴.
examples: The movement was not born in the boardrooms — it began in the streets.

drillQuestions.question: The idea was not _____ in a corporate office — it started in a small garage.

sentences.notes: "halls of Washington" vs "backyards, living rooms, front porches"의 대비. 권력의 중심(워싱턴) 대 평범한 미국인의 생활 공간을 대비. 세 지명(Des Moines, Concord, Charleston)은 각각 아이오와, 뉴햄프셔, 사우스캐롤라이나 예비선거의 핵심 도시.

koreanAlignment.ko: 우리의 선거 운동은 꾸며진 것이 아닙니다

koreanAlignment.en: it began in the backyards of Des Moines
and the living rooms of Concord
and the front porches of Charleston

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대비법(antithesis) — 워싱턴 권력가 vs 평범한 미국 가정의 공간적 대비

sentences.original: It was built by working men and women who dug into what little savings they had to give $5 and $10 and $20 to this cause.
sentences.translation: 그것은 가진 적은 저축에서 5달러, 10달러, 20달러를 이 대의를 위해 기부한 일하는 남녀에 의해 만들어졌습니다.

grammarElements.text: who dug into what little savings they had
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (who) + what절

grammarElements.text: to give $5 and $10 and $20 to this cause
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사)

vocabulary.example: She had to dig into her savings to pay for the repair.
They donated money to a worthy cause.
He used his life savings to start the business.

expressions.usage: "가진 것이 얼마 되지 않지만 그 전부를"이라는 뉘앙스. what + little의 결합.
평범한 노동자 계층을 지칭하는 정치적 표현

patterns.pattern: It was built by [people] who [sacrificial action]
patterns.explanation: 수동태로 평범한 사람들의 기여를 강조하는 패턴.
examples: It was achieved by volunteers who gave their time and energy.

drillQuestions.question: The movement was built _____ ordinary citizens who donated their time.

sentences.notes: "what little savings they had"에서 what + little은 "가진 적은 ~의 전부"라는 의미의 고급 표현. $5, $10, $20의 구체적 금액 나열이 소액 기부의 진정성을 강조. 관계대명사 who의 핵심 용법.

koreanAlignment.en: who dug into what little savings they had

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 구체적 나열(enumeration) — $5, $10, $20의 구체적 금액으로 풀뿌리 운동의 진정성을 강조

sentences.original: The road ahead will be long.

vocabulary.example: The road ahead is full of challenges.

patterns.explanation: "앞으로의 길은 ~할 것이다." 미래의 도전을 비유적으로 표현하는 패턴.
examples: The road ahead will be difficult, but we will persevere.

drillQuestions.question: The road ahead _____ be challenging, but we are ready.

sentences.notes: 짧은 문장으로 현실적 경고를 전달. 다음 문장과 함께 병렬 구조를 이루며 리듬감을 만든다.

vocabulary.example: The climb to the summit took five hours.
The road was too steep for most cars.

patterns.explanation: 앞 문장과 동일한 구조의 병렬 반복. 리듬감 있는 짧은 문장의 연속.
examples: Our journey will be long. Our task will be great.

drillQuestions.question: Our _____ will be steep, but we will reach the top.

sentences.notes: 앞 문장(The road ahead will be long)과 동일한 SVC 구조의 병렬. "climb"과 "steep"은 산을 오르는 이미지로 도전의 어려움을 시각화.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 병렬 구조(parallelism) — 앞 문장과 동일한 구조로 리듬과 강조

sentences.original: We may not get there in one year, or even one term, but America — I have never been more hopeful than I am tonight that we will get there.
sentences.translation: 1년 안에, 아니 한 임기 안에 도달하지 못할 수도 있습니다, 하지만 미국이여 — 저는 오늘 밤만큼 우리가 그곳에 도달할 것이라고 희망에 찬 적이 없었습니다.

grammarElements.text: in one year, or even one term
grammarElements.label: 시간 전치사구 (점층)

grammarElements.text: I have never been more hopeful than I am tonight
grammarElements.label: 주절 (현재완료 비교급)

vocabulary.example: The president served two terms.
We remain hopeful about the future.

expressions.usage: 앞의 표현보다 더 극단적인 가능성을 추가할 때

expressions.phrase: I have never been more ... than I am tonight
expressions.usage: 현재 순간의 감정이 최고임을 표현하는 최상급의 변형

patterns.pattern: We may not [achieve] in [short time], or even [longer time], but I have never been more [adjective]
patterns.explanation: 현실적 한계를 인정한 후 그럼에도 불구하고 더 큰 희망을 표현하는 양보-반전 패턴.
examples: We may not solve this in a day, or even a month, but I have never been more determined.

drillQuestions.question: I have never been _____ hopeful than I am right now.

sentences.notes: "America"는 호격(vocative)으로, 나라 전체를 직접 부르는 수사적 기법. "I have never been more hopeful than I am tonight"는 현재완료 비교급의 최상급적 용법. 현실 인정과 희망의 대비가 강렬.

koreanAlignment.en: in one year, or even one term
I have never been more hopeful
koreanAlignment.ko: 저는 이만큼 희망에 찬 적이 없었습니다

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 호격(apostrophe) + 양보-반전(concession-reversal) — 나라를 직접 부르며 현실 인정 후 희망으로 전환

sentences.original: I promise you: We as a people will get there.
sentences.translation: 여러분에게 약속합니다: 우리는 한 국민으로서 그곳에 도달할 것입니다.

vocabulary.example: I promise to do my best.
We must stand together as a people.

expressions.usage: 강한 다짐이나 서약을 할 때. 콜론(:)으로 약속의 내용을 도입.
개인이 아닌 집단적 정체성을 강조할 때

patterns.explanation: 청중에게 직접적으로 약속하는 패턴. 콜론(:) 뒤에 약속 내용을 배치.
examples: I promise you: We will not give up.
I promise you: Justice will be served.

drillQuestions.question: I _____ you: We will find a way.

sentences.notes: 짧지만 강렬한 약속. "We as a people"에서 "as a people"이 삽입되어 개인이 아닌 국민 전체의 의지를 강조. 콜론(:) 뒤의 짧은 문장이 약속의 무게를 높인다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 서약(pledge) — 지도자가 국민에게 직접 약속하는 정치 연설의 전형적 기법

sentences.original: This is our chance to answer that call.
sentences.translation: 이것은 그 부름에 응답할 우리의 기회입니다.

grammarElements.text: our chance to answer that call
grammarElements.label: 보어 (to부정사 수식)

vocabulary.example: This is your chance to make a difference.
She answered the call to serve.

expressions.usage: 사명이나 의무에 응하는 것을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.explanation: "이것은 ~할 우리의 ~이다." 행동을 촉구하는 짧고 힘있는 패턴.
examples: This is our chance to change the world.
This is our duty to protect the environment.

drillQuestions.question: This is our chance _____ make history.

sentences.notes: 마지막 단락의 삼중 반복(This is our...)의 시작. 점점 짧아지는 구조로 긴박감과 결의를 높인다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 삼중 반복 시작(tricolon opening) — "This is our..."의 반복으로 절정부 진입

vocabulary.example: This is a historic moment for the country.

patterns.explanation: 극도로 짧은 선언문. 반복 사용되어 리듬과 긴장감을 극대화.
examples: This is our time. This is our fight.

drillQuestions.question: This is _____ moment to stand up and be counted.

sentences.notes: 삼중 반복의 두 번째. 앞 문장보다 더 짧아지며 긴장감이 고조된다.
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층적 축약(escalating brevity) — 문장이 짧아지며 긴장감 극대화

sentences.original: This is our time — to put our people back to work and open doors of opportunity for our kids; to restore prosperity and promote the cause of peace; to reclaim the American Dream and reaffirm that fundamental truth that out of many, we are one; that while we breathe, we hope, and where we are met with cynicism, and doubt, and those who tell us that we can't, we will respond with that timeless creed that sums up the spirit of a people: Yes, we can.
sentences.translation: 이것은 우리의 시간입니다 — 우리 국민을 다시 일터로 보내고 아이들에게 기회의 문을 열어주기 위한; 번영을 회복하고 평화의 대의를 촉진하기 위한; 아메리칸 드림을 되찾고 다수로부터 하나라는 근본적 진리를 재확인하기 위한; 숨 쉬는 한 희망하며, 냉소와 의심과 우리가 할 수 없다고 말하는 사람들을 만나는 곳에서 한 민족의 정신을 요약하는 그 영원한 신조로 응답할 우리의 시간: 네, 우리는 할 수 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: to put our people back to work and open doors of opportunity
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사 ①)

grammarElements.text: to restore prosperity and promote the cause of peace
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사 ②)

grammarElements.text: to reclaim the American Dream and reaffirm that fundamental truth
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사 ③)

grammarElements.text: that out of many, we are one
grammarElements.label: 동격절 (that)

grammarElements.text: that while we breathe, we hope
grammarElements.label: 동격절 + while 종속절

grammarElements.text: and where we are met with cynicism, and doubt, and those who tell us that we can't
grammarElements.label: where 관계부사절 + who 관계대명사절

grammarElements.text: we will respond with that timeless creed that sums up the spirit of a people
grammarElements.label: 주절 + that 관계대명사절

vocabulary.example: The nation enjoyed a period of prosperity.
The leaders reaffirmed their commitment to peace.
Public cynicism about politics is growing.
The creed guided their actions.
We must reclaim our values.

expressions.phrase: out of many, we are one
expressions.usage: 미국의 국가 모토. 다양성 속의 통합을 표현.
라틴어 "dum spiro spero"의 영어 번역. 삶과 희망의 불가분 관계 표현.
시대를 초월하는 믿음이나 가치를 표현할 때
오바마 캠페인의 핵심 슬로건

patterns.pattern: This is our time — to [A]; to [B]; to [C] and reaffirm that [truth]
patterns.explanation: 선언문 + 대시 + to부정사의 삼중 반복으로 비전을 구체화하는 웅변적 패턴.
examples: This is our moment — to build bridges; to heal divisions; to create a future we can be proud of.

drillQuestions.question: This is our time — _____ rebuild, to renew, and to restore.

sentences.notes: 연설의 대미를 장식하는 최종 문장. to부정사 삼중 병렬 → that 동격절 → while/where 종속절 → 관계대명사 that → 슬로건 인용까지, 모든 수사적 기법이 총동원된 절정부. "E Pluribus Unum"(Out of many, one)은 미국 국장의 모토. "Yes, we can"은 캠페인 슬로건의 최종 등장.

koreanAlignment.en: to put our people back to work
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 국민을 다시 일터로 보내기 위한

koreanAlignment.en: and open doors of opportunity for our kids
koreanAlignment.ko: 그리고 아이들에게 기회의 문을 열어주기 위한

koreanAlignment.en: to restore prosperity and promote the cause of peace
koreanAlignment.ko: 번영을 회복하고 평화의 대의를 촉진하기 위한

koreanAlignment.en: that out of many, we are one
that while we breathe, we hope
where we are met with cynicism, and doubt
we will respond with that timeless creed

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 대단원(peroration) — 삼중 반복, 병렬, 점층, 인용 등 모든 수사 기법의 총결산. "Yes, we can"으로 청중 참여 극대화
